Description: Interesting sound at 4 seconds (possible humpback?) but otherwise there's mostly a mid-frequency hiss. Not sure what caused the false positives!
(SV: sounds like a humpback to me!)
Could it be the nearby humpback or a minke in addition to the srkws?
Sounds like faint humpback noises 7:15 AM
Not sure if orca or humpback, very faint call
Humpback song continues in quiet seas
Surprisingly high confidence and number of detections for what sounds like pretty white noise (static).
No ocean signal. Electrical noise comes and goes. Hydrophone may have been damaged during tonight's wind storm...
Human voices audible over ship noise. Not sure if they are from humans on beach or from radio interference...
False positives caused by human voices on 4th of July with element out of water.
Ship noise with high pitched whine
Jingling bell or key sounds with power peaks near 5.0, 5.6, and 9.5kHz. The source is unkown, currently.
No ocean signal. Hydrophone may have been damaged during tonight's wind storm...
Mostly static, but with some dropouts. Perhaps the Pi is failing or corrosion increasing somewhere?
Water sounds and swishing caused false positives. Later in clip there is tonal boat noise.
Periodic water and wave impact sounds with human voices on 4th of July in between. Sounds like element was mostly out of the water.

Unusual high-frequency tonal noise pattern (likely from a passing boat). In this clip it is nearly continuous with some dramatic modulations. This was a high-wind day in Puget Sound... and some sections have lower-frequency tones and modulations that sound a bit like wind whistling -- maybe when gusts blow through under the pier above the hydrophone?
Unusual harmonic signal with many harmonics all inaudible to me.  Not biophony, for sure.
Variable amplitude in 60Hz hum, plus some crackling.
More dropouts, increasing. Maybe damage from heavy rains last night? I'll stop stream.
Unusual high-frequency tonal noise pattern from passing boat, plus a couple jingles. This was a high-wind day in Puget Sound...
Short mid-frequency squeaks in tonal prop noise seem to be cause of these false positives.
Tonal boat noise with some swishing and lots of contact noise.
Human voices causing false positives despite significant noise from something contacting ladder and/or hydrophone.
Unusually complex high-frequency tones in what sounds like boat noise, including slow frequency modulation and starts/stops...
More jingles, and a few clank-like short ones that have harmonics or overtones to much higher frequencies.
Clear jingles repeated in series with periodic spacing of about a second.
Good calls and clicks, moderate ship noise
Good calls and clicks, high background noise
Good calls and clicks, some water slosh
Clear calls from J pod thru radio interference
Moderate to strong calls, high natural ambient noise
Good calls and clicks, loud ship noise
Faint to moderate calls and ambient noise
J pod calls clear through a small amount of radio interference.
Good calls and high natural ambient noise
Good calls and clicks, moderate ship noise
J pod calls during NB travel per WSSJI & ON.
Clear S03 calls with little background noise, other than a bit of radio interference.
Good calls and high natural ambient noise
Fair calls and clicks, loud ship noise
Impressive performance of the model, detecting almost all of the calls, despite intense low frequency noise from a passing train, as well as tonal train-whistles.
Good calls, some clicks, high natural ambient
Fair calls and clicks, loud ship noise
A few Bigg's calls, most likely from the T124A4s that are currently being tracked in San Juan Channel by local sighting networks (including OBI and Orca Network).
Good transient calls, lots of water noise
possible fain Bigg's call at 13:06 , T87 in the area.
Strong transient (Bigg's) calls, loud ship noise
Biggs call, T87 in area (SV: interesting sound, but not convinced it is a Bigg's call)
Calls of Bigg's killer whales, in one case with two calls overlapping.
Tonal vessel noise plus signals from likely Bigg's KWs or humpbacks...
More Biggs calls through some boat noise, plus some fish grunts around the 45sec mark.
Tonal vessel noise (sounds like a plane or LF outboard), plus signals likely from Bigg's but could be a humpback or something else.
A couple good transient calls, followed by increasing boat noise
Bigg's calls and some vessel noise
Bigg’s call repeated, one significantly more intense than others
Sounds like faint Bigg's calls to me, but SNR is low in vessel noise...
Fainter Bigg's calls but still audible
